claimText: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) releases the assessment report on Polluted River Stretches for Restoration of Water Quality.
evidenceExcerpt: Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) released assessment report on Polluted River Stretches for Restoration of Water Quality - 2025.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the organization responsible for publishing specific environmental reports.

claimText: The National Water Quality Monitoring Programme (NWMP) is executed by the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) in collaboration with State Pollution Control Boards and Committees.
evidenceExcerpt: CPCB executes National Water Quality Monitoring Programme (NWMP) with State Pollution Control Boards/Pollution Control Committees
examUse: Statement-based question on the implementing agencies of national environmental monitoring programs.
distractorHooks: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC) directly without state boards

claimText: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has been identifying the polluted river stretches in India since the year 2009.
evidenceExcerpt: has been identifying the country's Polluted River Stretches since 2009.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the timeline and historical mandate of CPCB's river monitoring initiatives.
distractorHooks: Since the enactment of the Water Act in 1974
Since the launch of the Namami Gange Programme
Identified by the Central Water Commission since 2014
